verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 1) is an ER membrane multipass channel releasing Ca2+ from ER lumen to cytoplasm. Its localization is ER (and some nuclear envelope continuous with ER). Checking contextual buckets: cell_state_induced — no documented surface display under stress/transformation; tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-restricted PM pool; lysosomal_exocytosis — not lysosomal; dual_localization — no validated PM pool, no shed ectodomain; stable_surface_attachment — not secreted. No surface biotinylation, flow, or therapeutic targeting the cell surface form. Classical ER-resident channel.
